source.title: Pinduoduo Merchant Economics, Refund-Only Policy Reform, and 100B Yuan Support Program

extractions.claim: Pinduoduo has the lowest all-in merchant cost among major Chinese e-commerce platforms: ~1% commission + performance ads
extractions.evidence: Platform take rate comparison: Pinduoduo 4-5% (of which ~1% is commission, rest is advertising). Taobao/Tmall 3-5% (setup fees + commissions). JD.com 8-10% (includes logistics). Douyin 5-10% (livestream service fees). Top Pinduoduo merchants spend 3-5x more on ads in 2025 vs 2022, showing rising ad monetization.

verification.verifier_notes: Take rates are industry estimates from multiple analyst and industry sources. Individual merchant experiences vary by category. The ~1% commission rate is from PDD's own platform documentation.

extractions.claim: Pinduoduo's refund-only policy was the most controversial merchant pain point — now being phased out across Chinese e-commerce
extractions.evidence: The 'refund-only' (仅退款) policy allowed consumers to get refunds without returning products. By July 2025, all major Chinese platforms — Pinduoduo, JD, Taobao, Douyin, Kuaishou — terminated refund-only policies after regulatory pressure. This was a significant source of merchant dissatisfaction, with merchants fearing consumer exploitation and higher costs.

verification.verifier_notes: Multiple news sources confirm the July 2025 policy change across all platforms.

extractions.claim: PDD launched 100 billion yuan merchant support program over 3 years, causing operating profit to drop 38% in Q1 2025
extractions.evidence: The Merchant Rights Protection Committee announced >RMB 100B in funds, traffic, and resources over 3 years. Specific measures: category guarantees reduced from 20,000 yuan to 500 yuan, promotion fee refund ratio increased to 3-4%, technical service fee reductions. Q1 2025 impact: operating profit fell 38% YoY, GAAP operating expenses surged 37%, stock fell 13.64% after earnings.

verification.verifier_notes: From PDD earnings release and multiple news sources. The 38% operating profit decline in Q1 2025 is confirmed from the earnings release.
